title: Middle English translations and continuations of Ranulf Higden's Polychronicon (Rolls Series edited selection)
summary: The repository artifact pins a U-M/CME encoding of selected Middle English material in the nine-volume Rolls Series edition: Trevisa's translation and short continuation, an independent anonymous translation, a Harley MS 1900 alternative, and selected Caxton adaptation/continuation text. The graph separates intellectual-version nodes, manuscript witnesses, one aggregate edition and nine printed volume expressions, nine IA/Google scan carriers, derivative OCR/scandata and metadata objects, the historical U-M page-image layer, the current Medictionary reformat, and supporting catalog or scholarly records.
review_notes: The schema has no abstract-work or abstract-version entity types. The Latin Polychronicon and the four English version-level nodes are represented as catalog_description entities solely as a schema-native compromise; their notes identify the abstraction explicitly.

Access and rights apply only to the named entity and component. Printed volume-expression rights, scan-carrier/provider terms, derivative-file rights, catalog-data licenses, manuscript text, and manuscript-image supply terms are not inherited across the graph.

The current U-M item exposes a viewer link, but an automated request to a sample image route returned HTTP 403. That does not prove direct public download or unavailability; the 5,176-image layer remains access status unknown and is not cached as a current image set.
Volume IX contains no target work text but remains in the complete nine-volume edition and is retained as target-absent boundary evidence.

entities.label: Pinned CME source XML for ahb1341
entities.description: The exact Kyvero-Press/CME XML artifact used to identify and model the repository work at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
notes: The header reports 5,176 600dpi TIFF G4 page images and item ID AHB1341.0001.001.
The cached copy is content-identical to the raw GitHub object at the pinned commit.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHB1341.0001.001
entities.description: The 2006 U-M digital text from which the pinned CME XML descends. Its item header describes OCR creation without correction or content editing.

entities.label: U-M linked page-image layer for AHB1341.0001.001
entities.description: The historical header reports 5,176 600dpi TIFF G4 page images and the XML contains 7,454 PB occurrences. The physical copies scanned are unidentified and a complete current public image set has not been verified.
entities.reproduction_format: 5,176 600dpi TIFF G4 images reported in the historical header
notes: The 7,454 PB occurrences reuse image references; they are not 7,454 unique images.
A landing-page viewer link plus HTTP 403 from one automated image-route check does not establish a direct-download status.

entities.label: Medictionary reformatted CME XML for ahb1341 (May 2026)
entities.description: A current reformatted XML distribution. Structural and text comparison found substantive target-stream identity apart from tiny editorial-note additions; it is a version of the historical U-M-derived encoding, not a separate edition.

entities.label: Ranulf Higden's Latin Polychronicon (abstract work)
entities.description: The Latin universal chronicle underlying the English translations and adaptations.
notes: Schema compromise: catalog_description represents an abstract work because the lineage schema has no abstract-work entity type.

entities.label: John Trevisa's Middle English Polychronicon translation and short continuation (abstract version)
notes: Schema compromise: catalog_description represents an abstract textual version because the schema has no abstract-version entity type.
The version includes the short continuation counted with the Trevisa family, not as another work.

entities.label: Anonymous second Middle English Polychronicon translation (abstract version)
entities.description: An independent complete seven-book translation, inferred by the Rolls editors to date probably c. 1432-1460 and surviving only in Harley MS 2261.

date_statements.label: probable translation date
date_statements.notes: This intellectual-version inference is distinct from the sole manuscript witness's catalog date of 1450-1474.

notes: Schema compromise: catalog_description represents an abstract textual version because the schema has no abstract-version entity type.

entities.label: Harley MS 1900 Book VI chapters 15-26 alternative (abstract version passage)
entities.description: A zero-count Trevisa-version child printed in volume VII as an alternative passage on pages [501]-538.
notes: Schema compromise: catalog_description represents an abstract version passage because the schema has no abstract-version entity type.
The source begins at Harley MS 1900 fol. 248v (Rolls 248b); the exact manuscript endpoint remains unresolved.

entities.label: William Caxton's 1482 Polycronicon adaptation and continuation (abstract version)
entities.description: Caxton's printed modernization and adaptation of Trevisa with continuation from 1357 through 1460.
notes: Schema compromise: catalog_description represents an abstract print version because the schema has no abstract-version entity type.
The exact Caxton copy or copies used by the Rolls editors remain unidentified.

entities.label: Cambridge, St John's College, MS H.1 (204)
entities.description: The principal Rolls Series base manuscript for Trevisa's translation.

notes: The selected Trevisa text begins approximately at fol. 34r and runs through approximately fol. 280r.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 24194
entities.description: An incomplete Trevisa witness collated by the Rolls editors.

notes: Main text fols. 36r-262rb; missing Book III chapter 41 through Book IV chapter 4, Book I chapters 1-4, and part of Book VI chapters 14-25.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1900
entities.description: A Trevisa witness and source of the alternative Book VI chapters 15-26 printed in the volume VII appendix.

holding.notes: No exact current BL public catalog record was verified on 2026-07-16; do not substitute an adjacent record.

notes: Main Trevisa text approximately fols. 44r-310v; the printed alternative begins at fol. 248v, but its endpoint is unresolved.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2261
entities.description: The sole known manuscript witness of the anonymous second translation.

notes: Table fols. 1r-16r; main narrative fols. 17r-445r.

entities.label: Rolls Series 41 Polychronicon edition, nine-volume aggregate
entities.description: The complete nine-volume scholarly edition published in London from 1865 to 1886. The target selection is present in volumes I-VIII and absent from volume IX.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple Trevisa witnesses, Harley MS 2261, and Caxton's printed version
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel Latin/English text with variant apparatus and a separate Harley MS 1900 appendix
editorial_practices.description: The Rolls editors establish the English texts from multiple manuscript and print sources rather than reproducing one carrier mechanically.

editorial_practices.representation: 17 English target DIV1 streams and 613 target DIV2 chapters; Latin parallel text and extensive front/back matter are represented chiefly as omitted placeholders
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item is an edited digital selection from the larger printed edition, not an encoding of all substantive Latin and English matter in all nine volumes.

evidence.summary: qpdf --check passed on all nine PDFs; PDF page counts are 552, 528, 536, 542, 572, 568, 618, 658, and 648; first, middle, and last pages rendered successfully for every volume. Target pages in volumes I, VII, and VIII were additionally inspected.
notes: The remaining PDF paths are source-cache/ahb1341/rolls-series-41-vol2-1869-ia.pdf through rolls-series-41-vol9-1886-ia.pdf.

open_questions.question: What exact folio in Harley MS 1900 ends the Book VI chapters 15-26 alternative printed on volume VII pages [501]-538?
next_steps: Inspect the manuscript or obtain an authoritative folio-level description; no enquiry has been sent.

open_questions.question: Which physical copies supplied U-M's 5,176 page images, and what current direct image-delivery route, if any, is publicly usable?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections staff to identify scan-copy provenance and current image delivery; no enquiry has been sent.

open_questions.question: Which exact Caxton 1482 copy or copies did the Rolls Series editors use for the selected readings and continuation?
next_steps: Review all Rolls Series introductions and collation sigla against surviving ISTC holdings; no holding institution has been contacted.

open_questions.question: How should the collection-level manual-keying/proofreading account be reconciled with this item's header statement that it was OCR-created without corrections or content editing?
next_steps: Seek item-level production documentation from U-M; no enquiry has been sent.
